outcomes.Definition: Appointment was successfully scheduled. Job type breakdown (HVAC, plumbing, etc.) is a separate dimension and not part of reason/sub-reason.
outcomes.Technical: New job created in CRM by the Netic Agent

outcomes.Definition: Earliest available appointment is too far out for the customer
Customer needed emergency service but none was available
Other scheduling conflicts — e.g. customer can only do weekends or late afternoons
Customer abandoned the call after learning the price

outcomes.Reason: Need to speak to third party
outcomes.Definition: Customer wants to check with a family member before committing
Customer decided to go to a competitor due to pricing
Customer decided to go to a competitor due to timing
Customer decided to go to a competitor for other/unclear reasons

outcomes.Subreason: Will Try to Solve on Their Own
outcomes.Definition: Customer decided to solve the problem DIY or via friend, family, or neighbor
Customer refused to continue with AI and no transfer was completed
Booking failed due to trouble confirming the customer's details (address, name, etc.)
Customer dropped off citing frustration with the number of questions
Customer showed general frustration
Call ended without a clear reason — no objection signal identified
outcomes.Technical: No objection classifier fired on an unbooked call

outcomes.Definition: Caller's goal was to gather pricing info with no intent to book

outcomes.Reason: Need to speak to third party
outcomes.Definition: Caller needs landlord sign-off; treated as Handled since it's a service-blocking constraint, not just a timing issue

outcomes.Reason: Need to speak to third party
outcomes.Definition: Caller cannot authorize the booking themselves
Customer intended to book but is outside the service area
Customer intended to book but the job or trade is not supported
Customer confirmed their upcoming appointment time
Agent successfully moved an existing appointment to a new date or time
Customer checked on their appointment and asked about its details
Customer added additional info for their upcoming appointment
Cancelled existing appointment due to scheduling conflicts (e.g. sick, family emergency, other appointment)
Cancelled existing appointment and went with a competitor for pricing reasons
Cancelled existing appointment and went with a competitor for timing reasons
Cancelled existing appointment and went with a competitor for other/unclear reasons
Cancelled existing appointment because the customer solved it themselves
Cancelled existing appointment because the problem fixed itself
Cancelled existing appointment because they could not afford it (cost too high, financing declined, insurance issues, etc.)
Cancelled for other/unknown reason
outcomes.Technical: Intent classifier (no specific objection signal on cancellation)

outcomes.Definition: Customer inquired about business hours or locations served
Customer inquired about technician qualifications or trades serviced
Caller inquired about business partnerships or referral arrangements
Customer inquired about insurance or payment options
Customer asked billing questions
Customer asked membership questions. Modifying membership is live transfer; getting membership details is TAKE A NOTE
Customer asked warranty questions
Customer asked about permits, certifications, or other government-related questions
Caller inquiring about a career opportunity
The call is spam or irrelevant — gibberish, prank calls, sales/telemarketing, auto-reply, or unrelated to the business
Caller called the wrong number
Caller is a salesperson / telemarketer
Call between two internal phone numbers (employees or technicians)
Call had no significant interaction but doesn't fit the above
No meaningful interaction occurred — the call had no transcript or the caller provided no substantive response
outcomes.Technical: No transcript recorded, or intent classifier returns no meaningful interaction

outcomes.Definition: Caller hung up quickly after indicating they would call back
outcomes.Technical: Intent classifier (excused — callback) with no transfer completed

outcomes.Definition: Caller immediately asked for a human with no booking context, and no transfer was completed
outcomes.Technical: Intent classifier (excused — requested representative) with no transfer completed

transfers: Definition
Customer requested a human because they explicitly prefer humans, or do so immediately before significant conversation occurs
Customer requested a supervisor, manager, or someone higher up to talk to
Customer requested a human because they were getting frustrated with AI
Customer requested a human for an unknown reason
Caller missed a call from CXR / technician and is following up

Caller specifically missed a call for rescheduling a job
- Generic rescheduling outbound
- Maintenance rescheduling outbound, if configured
- Maintenance rescheduling outbound with an offer for credit, if configured
Customer asked billing questions
Customer asked membership questions. Modifying membership is live transfer, getting membership details is TAKE A NOTE
Customer asked warranty questions
Customer asked permits, certifications, and other government related questions
Customer asked insurance questions
Customer needs to share sensitive info
Process is defined to escalate inquired job type
Job type not handled by Netic

Custom rules, e.g.
- fireplace service
- geothermal service
- appliance service
- duct work
- etc.
Caller requested service for a commercial property or location
Postal codes that require escalation zones

Caller mentioned a mobile home or other non-serviceable property type
- mobile home
- stacked / side-by-side condo
AI agent faced issues validating the customer's address against the database
Customer is calling for a rental inspection

Custom rules: e.g.
- Meant to call other branch
No availabilities were found
Appointment had transfer criteria, e.g. attached to a dummy technician
Appointment confirmation requires transfer
Other custom rules, e.g. specific third party leads, etc
Customer has "Do Not Service" tag
Customer has escalation tag
Other custom rules, e.g. HOA

Customer has an inquiry about parts
- If customized, we may break out certain types of parts orders, e.g. filter changes and refrigerant recharges
Caller following up on an open estimate
Caller following up regarding in-progress work
Caller following up on recent install
TAKE A NOTE. Caller is reporting forgotten tools and equipment by technician.
AI agent faced issues validating the customer's name against the database
AI agent found no appointments for a customer who claimed they had an appointment scheduled
AI agent found an appointment booked for the past, but not in-progress/completed
Customer is trying to modify an appointment where a tech has already been dispatched
Customer is trying to book a duplicate booking
Customer is trying to modify an appointment, but is a new address or customer
Issue with mismatched info for reschedule request
Customer requested two appointments to be booked
Customer is asking to reinstate their already cancelled appointment
Customer requests to make a significant change to existing appointment. E.g. entirely new trade or entirely new address.
Customer is a non-owner/occupant of their service location
Customer requests to be contacted via different modality (call, text, email, etc.)
Customer explicitly asks for a follow up
Customer previously had a bad experience with business including injury or damage.
Customer previously had a bad experience with business during a past appointment or interaction. Can include more specific custom rules like "threatening to end relationship" if configured
Customer exhibits repeated frustration during conversation
Caller wants to report a driver/truck related to the company
Customer wants to be contacted via call
Customer wants to be contacted via email
Customer wants to be contacted via text
Customer requests Spanish
Customer booked but wanted a sooner appt if one becomes available
Caller inquiring about a career opportunity.
Technician or other existing employee calls CSR agent.
Caller is at the office.
TAKE A NOTE. Customer asks for guidance on using equipment.
Customer wants all unbooked calls to be flagged. Was noted as "transfer" for a while, but then we stopped doing so by setting mark_transfer = false
Custom high value job flag. Similar to Unbooked, was marked as a transfer for a while
Custom rule to transfer any no interaction calls
Technical issue, e.g. max API tries for most routes, RR 500s, etc. We track more specific reasons, but bucket them together when showing to the customer
Custom escalation rule. This should be dead and broken out, but is here for historic reasons
Did not fit any other category
